title: The Slow Train to Edinburgh
summary: A traveller describes choosing the slow scenic train from London to Edinburgh and what he sees and learns along the seven-hour journey.
body: Last March, I took the slow train from London to Edinburgh. The fast service is only four and a half hours, but the slow train takes nearly seven. I chose the slow one on purpose. I wanted to see the small towns and the coast, not just dark tunnels and fields. The train left King's Cross at nine in the morning. The first part was quiet — only a few people in my carriage. After York, the route follows the eastern coast for a long time. The sea was grey but calm, and there were small fishing boats close to the shore. I bought a cheese sandwich and a hot tea from the trolley. I should have brought a book, but I had forgotten one at home. I ended up just looking out of the window. Around lunchtime, a young father sat opposite me with his daughter. She was about four years old and very excited about the sheep in the fields. She tried to count them, but she always lost track after twelve. When the train finally stopped in Edinburgh, it was almost four o'clock. I was tired, but in a soft, happy way. The slow train didn't only carry me; it slowed my mind down too, which was probably the real reason I had paid for the ticket.

annotations.exampleEn: The fast service costs more than the slow one.
I left early on purpose to avoid traffic.
We drove along the eastern coast for hours.
Small fishing boats moved slowly on the water.

annotations.meaningVi: xe đẩy bán đồ ăn nhẹ trên tàu
annotations.exampleEn: The trolley passed by twice during the trip.

annotations.phrase: I should have brought a book
annotations.meaningVi: đáng lẽ tôi nên mang theo một cuốn sách
annotations.exampleEn: I should have brought a book to read on the train.
She spent the trip looking out of the window.
I lost track of time while reading.

annotations.meaningVi: theo một cách dịu nhẹ và vui vẻ
annotations.exampleEn: She smiled in a soft, happy way.

annotations.meaningVi: làm chậm tâm trí tôi lại
annotations.exampleEn: Walking in the park slowed my mind down.

grammarNotes.title: Comparatives of time and speed
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng cấu trúc 'A is shorter/longer/faster than B' để so sánh thời gian hay tốc độ. Trong bài: 'The fast service is only four and a half hours, but the slow train takes nearly seven.' Đây là so sánh hai phương án.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use comparative structures to compare time or speed. From the text: 'The fast service is only four and a half hours, but the slow train takes nearly seven.'

grammarNotes.title: Past simple narrative chain
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể chuỗi sự việc theo thứ tự. Trong bài có hàng loạt động từ quá khứ đơn: 'I took', 'I chose', 'The train left', 'I bought', 'She tried', 'I was tired'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use Past Simple to narrate a series of events in order. The text uses many past simple verbs in sequence: 'I took', 'I chose', 'The train left', 'I bought', 'She tried', 'I was tired'.

translationVi: Tháng Ba vừa rồi, tôi đi chuyến tàu chậm từ London đến Edinburgh. Chuyến tàu nhanh chỉ mất bốn tiếng rưỡi, nhưng tàu chậm mất gần bảy tiếng. Tôi chọn tàu chậm có chủ đích. Tôi muốn ngắm những thị trấn nhỏ và bờ biển, chứ không chỉ là đường hầm tối và cánh đồng. Tàu rời ga King's Cross lúc chín giờ sáng. Đoạn đầu rất yên — toa tôi chỉ có vài người. Sau ga York, tàu đi men theo bờ biển phía Đông một quãng dài. Biển xám nhưng êm, có những chiếc thuyền đánh cá nhỏ áp sát bờ. Tôi mua một chiếc bánh sandwich pho mát và một cốc trà nóng từ xe đẩy. Đáng lẽ tôi nên mang theo một cuốn sách, nhưng tôi đã quên ở nhà. Cuối cùng tôi chỉ nhìn ra cửa sổ. Quãng giờ trưa, một ông bố trẻ ngồi đối diện tôi cùng con gái. Cô bé khoảng bốn tuổi và rất thích thú với đàn cừu ngoài cánh đồng. Bé cố đếm chúng, nhưng cứ đếm đến mười hai là lại quên mất. Khi tàu cuối cùng dừng ở Edinburgh, đã gần bốn giờ chiều. Tôi mệt, nhưng theo một kiểu nhẹ nhàng, vui vẻ. Chuyến tàu chậm không chỉ chở tôi đi; nó còn làm chậm tâm trí tôi lại, đó có lẽ mới là lý do thật sự tôi đã mua vé.

questions.prompt: When did the journey take place?
questions.explanation: 'Last March, I took the slow train from London to Edinburgh.'
questions.hint: Listen for the month at the very start.

questions.prompt: How long does the fast train take?
questions.explanation: 'The fast service is only four and a half hours.'
questions.hint: Listen for the fast service time.

questions.prompt: Why did he choose the slow train?
options: He wanted to see small towns and the coast
questions.explanation: 'I wanted to see the small towns and the coast, not just dark tunnels and fields.'

questions.prompt: From which station did the train leave?
questions.explanation: 'The train left King's Cross at nine in the morning.'
questions.hint: Listen for a London station name.

questions.prompt: What did he see along the eastern coast?
options: A grey but calm sea with small fishing boats
questions.explanation: 'The sea was grey but calm, and there were small fishing boats close to the shore.'
questions.hint: Listen for a colour adjective and a kind of boat.

questions.prompt: What did he buy from the trolley?
options: A coffee and a chocolate bar
A cheese sandwich and a hot tea
questions.explanation: 'I bought a cheese sandwich and a hot tea from the trolley.'
questions.hint: Listen for one food item and one drink.

questions.prompt: What had he forgotten at home?
questions.explanation: 'I should have brought a book, but I had forgotten one at home.'
questions.hint: Listen for what he wishes he had brought.

questions.prompt: Who sat opposite him around lunchtime?
options: An old woman with her dog
A young father with his daughter
questions.explanation: 'a young father sat opposite me with his daughter.'
questions.hint: Listen for two people sitting opposite him.

questions.prompt: What was the little girl excited about?
questions.explanation: 'very excited about the sheep in the fields.'

questions.prompt: Until what number could she count the sheep before losing track?
questions.hint: Listen for the highest number she managed.

questions.prompt: What does the narrator say was the real reason he paid for the slow ticket?
options: Because the slow train slowed his mind down too
questions.explanation: 'it slowed my mind down too, which was probably the real reason I had paid for the ticket.'

cloze.template: Last March, I took the slow {{b1}} from London to Edinburgh. The fast service is only four and a half hours, but the slow train takes nearly seven. I chose the slow one on {{b2}}. I wanted to see the small towns and the coast, not just dark tunnels and fields. The train left King's Cross at nine in the morning. The first part was {{b3}} — only a few people in my carriage. After York, the route follows the eastern {{b4}} for a long time. The sea was grey but calm, and there were small fishing {{b5}} close to the shore. I bought a cheese sandwich and a hot tea from the {{b6}}. I should have brought a book, but I had forgotten one at home. I ended up just looking out of the {{b7}}. Around lunchtime, a young father sat opposite me with his daughter. She was about four years old and very excited about the {{b8}} in the fields. She tried to count them, but she always lost {{b9}} after twelve. When the train finally stopped in Edinburgh, it was almost four o'clock. I was tired, but in a soft, happy way. The slow train didn't only carry me; it slowed my mind {{b10}} too, which was probably the real reason I had paid for the ticket.

blanks.explanation: 'train' — the main subject of the whole story.
'trolley' — the cart that sells food on a train.
'window' — what he watched the landscape through.
'sheep' — the animal the child was counting.
'track' — 'lose track' means to forget where you were in a count.
'down' — 'slow down' is a common phrasal pattern.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says the slow train 'slowed his mind down'. Have you ever chosen a slower way to do something on purpose — walking instead of driving, cooking instead of ordering? What did you gain from the slower choice, and what did you lose?
